content:
type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: I'll own both shared files (both our changes land in exif_transpose/test_exif_transpose). Please send a SPEC for strip_metadata so I can implement it alongside transform_callback.

content:
type: DEFER
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py
spec: Add optional keyword-only parameter transform_callback to exif_transpose(image, *, in_place=False, transform_callback=None). Signature: transform_callback: Callable[[int], Image.Transpose | Sequence[Image.Transpose] | None] | None = None. Behavior: after computing orientation = image_exif.get(ExifTags.Base.Orientation, 1), if transform_callback is not None, call method = transform_callback(orientation) INSTEAD of the built-in orientation->method dict lookup (the callback fully overrides the default mapping - default dict is only used when transform_callback is None). The callback's return value may be: (a) a single Image.Transpose enum value - apply exactly like the existing single-method code path (image.transpose(method) / image.im.transpose(method)), or (b) a sequence (list/tuple) of Image.Transpose values - apply them in order, each via successive .transpose() calls (for in_place, apply each transpose in sequence, reassigning image.im and image._size after each; for not-in_place, start from image and chain transpose() calls in order to build transposed_image), or (c) None - meaning no transformation should be applied (skip the transpose branch entirely, equivalent to method is None in the existing code), even if the raw orientation tag lookup would otherwise have produced a method. After the transform(s) are applied (or skipped), the existing behavior must be unchanged: strip the Orientation exif tag and update exif_image.info exactly as today. Edge cases: transform_callback receives the raw integer orientation value (whatever getexif() returned, including 1 or any value not in the standard dict, e.g. could be 0 or 9+, since callback may implement custom handling for values the default mapping doesn't cover) - do not restrict/validate the orientation value before passing it to the callback. If transform_callback is None (default), behavior must be 100% identical to current implementation. Update the docstring to document the new transform_callback parameter, its signature/purpose, and that it overrides the default orientation mapping.
note: transform_callback overrides method lookup in exif_transpose; supports single Transpose, sequence of Transpose, or None

After the transform(s) are applied (or skipped), the existing behavior must be unchanged: strip the Orientation exif tag and update exif_image.info exactly as today. If transform_callback is None (default), behavior must be 100% identical to current implementation. fields.note: transform_callback overrides method lookup in exif_transpose; supports single Transpose, sequence of Transpose, or None
